body_txt: Bad breath is a common occurrence both in real life and in media. Whether it's to the detriment of the sufferer, those around them or both, it's a ripe opportunity for writers to add in some good old Toilet Humour . As per the title, it often takes the form of a Stock Phrase where one character tells another to take a mint, brush their teeth or use some mouthwash. This doesn't always work though, as particularly oblivious characters may wonder why everyone offers them mints all the time - they don't even like them! Bad breath is often a temporary occurence, being used as a one-off gag or a single-episode issue. This is usually caused by character eating or drinking something strong smelling such as garlic, onions, cheese or coffee. However, it can also be a permanent character trait, especially for characters who have poor hygiene in general . It's common for bad breath to be the basis of a Stink Snub , even when the target has no such problem. Characters may use an Anticipatory Breath Spray to try to avert this. Halitosis can be used to establish an unusual or disgusting diet. Villains who eat endangered animals or even human may have a distinctive taint to their breath, while gluttons may have overly sweet breath from eating too much sugar. Animalistic characters may have rancid breath after eating raw or rotting meat, as anyone who's ever owned a dog will know all too well. Bad breath is commonly given as one of the reasons that Smoking Is Not Cool . This is Truth in Television , as smoking really does make your breath smell strongly of cigarette smoke. A smell of stale alcohol on the breath is also one of the telltale signs of The Alcoholic . Bad breath is sometimes associated with dragons and other creatures with a Breath Weapon , particularly poisonous ones. Such creatures may spew out a foul-smelling Deadly Gas , or their breath may be so powerful that it can incapacitate an opponent through smell alone . Characters with stinky breath may have the nickname "dragon breath", even if there are no actual dragons involved. In a bit of artistic license , bad breath is sometimes depicted as flammable in the same way as flatulence , despite this being impossible in real life. Flatulence is flammable because it contains hydrogen and/or methane, which build up in the gut and are released through farting. Bad breath involves many of the same bacterial processes as flatulence, but any flammable gases that are produced will quickly disperse from the mouth, and so they never accumulate to the point of becoming flammable. May be represented as a Visible Odor . Compare Smelly Feet Gag for another body part that's associated with smelling bad.
